objective: Objectif de stratégie crypto: En utilisant la famille robuste de signaux detectés (breakout + momentum), nous allons explorer les combinaisons peu courantes d'indicateurs comme EMA + OBV et BOLLINGER + STOCHASTIC. Ainsi, sur 0GUSDC en 1h, avec un SL à +/-3% volatilité de la liquidite (filtre horaire), nous allons tester une combinaison d'indicateurs aleatoires imposées pour ajouter un filtre de session/horaire et liquidite. Notre entrée sera EMA(10) + OBV avec BOLLINGER[20] + STOCHASTIC (K% 7,3). Nous allons mesurer le risque en utilisant l'ATR comme stop-loss à -5%, puis TP a 2%.

iterations.hypothesis: Using a combination of momentum and Bollinger bands with ATR based risk management, we aim to filter out noise in the market and find high probability trading opportunities.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] ITERATION 1/10
Objective: Objectif de stratégie crypto:
Using the robust family of detected signals (breakout + momentum), we will explore less common combinations of indicators such as EMA + OBV and BOLLINGER + STOCHASTIC. Therefore, on 0GUSDC in 1h with a SL at +/-3% volatility (hourly filter), we will test imposed random combinations for adding a session/hourly filter and liquidity. Our entry will be EMA(10) + OBV with BOLLINGER[20] + STOCHASTIC (K% 7,3). We'll measure risk using ATR as stop-loss at -5%, then TP at 2%.
Market: 0GUSDC 4h (851 bars)
Hypothesis: Using a combination of momentum and Bollinger bands with ATR based risk management, we aim to filter out noise in the market and find high probability trading opportunities.
Indicators: ema, obv, bollinger, atr
Entry long: ema(10) crosses above ema(20) AND obv > 0 AND bollinger[20].middle < close
Exit: close crosses above bollinger.middle OR close crosses below bollinger.lower AND atr > stoploss AND atr < target
[Backup plan] If results are poor (negative return), I would try to change the entry criteria by adjusting the EMA(10) and OBV parameters or remove one of them. If results are mediocre (low Sharpe ratio), I would try to adjust the Bollinger Bands parameter K, ATR stop-loss percentage, TP target, or add a filter for session/hourly based liquidity.

iterations.hypothesis: Ajustement structurel basé sur le diagnostic précédent.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy uses robust trading signals and uncommon combinations of indicators such as EMA + OBV and BOLLINGER + STOCHASTIC on 0GUSDC in the 4h timeframe. It has a high risk tolerance with an ATR stop-loss at -5% and target profit at 2%. The entry condition is <EMA10 and OBV> BOLLINGER.MIDDLE AND STOCHASTIC K<7>. Exit occurs when either EMA10 crosses BOLLINGER.MIDDLE or ATR is lower than the stop-loss. During iteration 2, the sharpe ratio was 0.685 with a total of 19 trades and +15.99% return.
[Backup plan] {'Weaknesses': 'Possible weaknesses include overfitting due to uncommon combination of indicators, high risk tolerance and lack of diversification.'}

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The strategy is based on robust breakout and momentum signals, coupled with EMA+OBV and Bollinger+Stochastic indicators. It aims to incorporate random combinations of indicators for improved performance. The entry long condition is satisfied if the higher-rated RSI and EMA are confirmed by a stronger momentum indicator, while risk management involves using ATR as stop loss at -5% and target profit at 2%. If results are poor (negative return), I would consider adjusting the stop loss to better manage risk. For mediocre performance (low Sharpe ratio), parameter adjustments could be made but further testing may be required. In case of failure, an alternative approach based on a different set of technical indicators could be explored.
[Backup plan] ['1) Adjusting stop-loss to reduce risk exposure', '2) Further refining parameters and backtesting', '3) Considering additional technical indicators for the strategy']

pre_reflection.text:

Exit occurs when the opposite signal or invalidation in momentum is received. Recent history shows positive results with Sharpe ratio of 0.685, but it's still running now.
[Backup plan] If results are poor (negative return), we would need to investigate why this happened. We could potentially try different combinations of indicators, adjust stop-loss and take profit parameters or change the entry condition based on market conditions. pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The strategy seems to have shown promising results so far, with an average sharpe ratio of 0.685 and a total return of +15.99%. However, it's important to continue monitoring the backtest as it progresses and make necessary adjustments if needed. Additionally, using different combinations of indicators like EMA+OBV and BOLLINGER+STOCHASTIC can help improve its performance. It would be wise to have a fallback plan in case the current strategy fails.
[Backup plan] If results are poor (negative return), we could try adjusting the stop-loss parameters, such as using an ATR with a tighter range or increasing the number of bars it considers when calculating volatility. We can also experiment with different combinations of indicators to see if they produce better results. If results are mediocre (low Sharpe ratio), we could look into optimizing our entry and exit criteria by adjusting our profit target, stop-loss, and other parameters.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] I am aware of potential weaknesses in this strategy such as overfitting due to extensive use of technical indicators, and the reliance on breakout signals which may not always be reliable. In case results are poor (negative return), I would consider adding a volatility filter or incorporating additional fundamental analysis into the strategy. For mediocre returns (low Sharpe ratio), adjustments could include using different combinations of moving averages or incorporating sentiment data. If this approach fails, an alternative would be to explore trend-following strategies that rely less on technical indicators and more on price action.
[Backup plan] If results are poor, I will consider adding a volatility filter to the strategy to reduce overfitting. Additionally, I may incorporate additional fundamental analysis into the strategy such as news sentiment or macroeconomic data. If results are mediocre, adjustments could include experimenting with different combinations of moving averages and incorporating more robust risk management techniques like using proper position sizing based on account size.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The strategy uses robust signals and uncommon combinations of indicators to identify trading opportunities. It has shown promising results so far with an Sharpe ratio of 0.685 in the last three iterations. However, it could be vulnerable to unexpected market movements or changes in market conditions that were not accounted for in its design.
[Backup plan] If the strategy performs poorly (negative returns), we would consider implementing a fallback contract that generates automatically when our exit limit order is not met. This can help maintain progress even if the primary strategy fails to generate profitable trades. Alternatively, we could explore other strategies or indicators to supplement this one and improve its performance.
